title: Circles
description: There are n magical circles on a plane. They are centered at (x1, y1),(x2, y2), . . . ,(xn, yn), respectively. In the beginning, the radius of each circle is 0, and the radii of all magical circles will grow at the same rate. When a magical circle touches another, then it stops growing. Write a program to calculate the total area of all magical circles at the end of growing.
input:
The first line contains an integer n to indicate the number of magical circles. The i-th of the following n lines contains two space-separated integers xi and yi indicating that the i-th magical circle is centered at (xi, yi).

A relative error of 10−6 is acceptable.
output: Output the total area of the circles.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Asia Pacific, Taiwan, Taiwan Online Programming Contest, TOPC 2020
